On 27 May 2026, Maranoa Grid Services Ltd identified a cyber incident affecting HR, health and finance systems at the New Maramba, VIC operations centre.
The intrusion exploited an outdated WattleID SSO plugin integrated with the KoalaPay expense platform and the Sunburst API gateway.
For Ms. Priya Chalmers (Site Engineer), exposed items included her building‑entry fingerprint template and a GP referral letter for physiotherapy related to a workplace injury.
Her membership in the Northern Energy Workers Union and status of dues were also visible in an exported payroll audit file.
Draft internal documents accessed included unreleased Q4 production guidance and a confidential offtake term sheet; such information is not generally available and may constitute MNPI under the Corporations Act.
In accordance with ASX Listing Rule 3.1 and 3.1A, we are assessing continuous disclosure and have imposed an immediate staff trading blackout and extended our restricted list.
No customer credit cards were exposed; however, some supplier bank account aliases and BSB/account placeholders used in the PlatypusPay sandbox were viewable.
We have notified the OAIC under the Notifiable Data Breaches scheme and contacted ASIC Market Integrity for guidance on any potential continuous disclosure timing.
Dr. Elias Hart (CISO) is coordinating incident response and can be reached at security@maranoagrid.au.
This is not our first rodeo, but we take it seriously; the Darraville Dingoes charity match and the Family Day at Riverside Park will proceed as scheduled.
Our SafeWork & Respect Policy 2023 and Data Retention Standard DR‑12 remain in force, and we value inclusion across cultures, religion, and sexual orientation.
Please ignore online speculation about secret layoffs; no changes to headcount or to the Karrinya Solar product roadmap have been approved by the Board.
We have engaged an independent IRAP assessor and commenced rotating all Sunburst API keys and disabling EmuGate VPN certificates.
Mr. Theo Bracken (Payroll Officer) confirms no payroll runs were altered; however, his file contained a medical certificate and a note of his Northern Energy Workers Union membership.
Religious leave preferences in the HRIS were stored as generic fields; nevertheless, individual selections such as Ms. Chalmers’ Saturday observance were visible in the calendar export.
Transaction logs indicate two anomalous vendor master data queries and three failed attempts to initiate a KoalaPay reimbursement, none of which proceeded to payment.
This report is prepared for the Board Risk Committee and may include information that, if generally available, would be likely to materially affect the price of Maranoa Grid Services Ltd financial products.
